search_queries_used: site:asahq.org statement documentation anesthesia care preanesthesia consent risk discussion
site:rcoa.ac.uk perioperative shared decision consent anesthesia
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Consent must reflect a real patient-specific discussion and applicable UAE law and policy.
A generic risk list cannot establish that a risk was relevant, explained, understood, or accepted.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Patients receiving anesthesia care when a qualified clinician completes a patient-specific risk and consent discussion.
setting_applicability: Preanesthesia assessment and consent documentation; emergency exceptions, capacity, proxy, and paediatric consent require separate rules.
UAE_applicability: ASA documentation evidence requires UAE consent, capacity, guardian, language, privacy, and facility-policy adaptation.
recency_verification: Current ASA 2023 documentation statement was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer ASA anesthesia-documentation statement was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The ASA section supports recording patient-specific risk discussion, questions, consent status, alternatives, and the responsible clinician-entered plan; it does not generate risks, consent, or a plan.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

unresolved_source_gaps: Procedure, technique, patient-specific benefits and risks, alternatives, no-treatment option, capacity, decision maker, interpreter, questions, understanding, consent form, signature, date, emergency exception, and final plan remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
